data.body: FIXED + VERIFIED. The unified write lock rebar._store.lock acquires BOTH legs (fcntl.flock + mkdir, dual_window=True) in a fixed order, so all same-clone writers mutually exclude on every platform class (flock(1), fcntl-only, mkdir-fallback) — the mkdir leg is ALWAYS taken, closing the bash-mkdir-vs-python-fcntl gap. Tier E E3 relocated the transition/claim critical section (ticket_txn.py -> rebar._commands.txn) and ported compact, all of which acquire via lock.acquire(dual_window=True); the dual-leg discipline was preserved and is now the sole write-lock path for Python (the only live impl). Regression coverage: tests/integration/test_store_concurrency.py (writer storm: 16 concurrent comments, zero loss, fsck clean; claim storm: 1 winner + N-1 exit-10) — both green. AC #1 (one lock serializes all writers every platform) and #2 (storm regression, no index.lock failures / lost commits) satisfied; #3 (land by Tier D) — dual-leg landed in Tier D and is preserved here.
